topic: Post-patch review of WIN-058b detail_evidence — confirms 2 decider patches (ISS-1158 status-downgrade rewording, ISS-1159 three->at-least-three hedge) landed, flags residual Pattern-paragraph internal inconsistency and small redundancy in the credit paragraph.
our_argument_summary: WIN-058b's detail_evidence was patched on 2026-04-18 (commit a0f217d) to resolve two moderate holes raised in WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181: (a) replaced the unsupported 'downgraded the κ claim from the headline confirmed tally to pending' with 'the new claim_key registers this entry as pending_independent_verification from the outset, with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required' (ISS-1158), and (b) changed 'Three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window' to 'At least three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window' to accommodate the ai_manifest.json registry_semantics fourth instance (ISS-1159). Both patches are present in the current wins.json (claim length 50, finding length 503, detail_evidence 5813, detail_verdict 948; verdict unchanged: Not Demonstrated). The core WIN critique (post-hoc ratio, Monte Carlo rebuttal of r=0.992, no derives_from_dome, no monitoring) is unchanged by either patch and remains defensible. However, the ISS-1159 hedge was applied narrowly — only to the trailing summary sentence — and left an internal inconsistency in the Pattern paragraph: the opening now reads 'This is the third acknowledge-without-fix move' (definite count of three) while the closing reads 'At least three documentary concessions' (hedged). WIN-058 received a fuller pass for the same issue (both its 'at least three instances in commit 6e06efb' heading and 'one of at least three parallel documentary concession' body were hedged). WIN-058b was not given the same coverage. A secondary issue: the ISS-1158 rewording, while factually safer than the prior 'downgraded' framing, introduced mild redundancy — 'the new claim_key registers this entry as pending_independent_verification from the outset, with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required' restates two facts already given two sentences earlier ('keyed win-058-bedrock-kappa (status: pending_independent_verification...)' and the verbatim honest_caveat quotation). Readable but slightly tautological; 'We credit both moves' then strains because move 2 is really a restatement of facts from the preceding API-description sentences rather than a distinct second concession.

relevant_files: data/wins.json — current WIN-058b entry (fresh clone HEAD 6d2a80b4, data/wins.json at a0f217d)
monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181.json — upstream batch review that filed the holes (queue_id 177)
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json — ISS-1157/1158/1159/1160 patch records (via commit a0f217d)
git diff a0f217d~1 a0f217d -- data/wins.json — verified both patches applied verbatim per recommendation
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py / pull_data.py pipeline for WIN-058b (κ coupling constant). The patches are prose-only edits inside detail_evidence; no code_analysis tag changes, no aggregate count changes, no predictions.json reach. detail_evidence_length grew from 5752 (batch review fingerprint) to 5813 (+61 chars) — consistent with 'Three'->'At least three' (+9) and 'the author downgraded the κ claim from the headline \'confirmed\' tally to pending inside the same commit' (98 chars) -> 'the new claim_key registers this entry as pending_independent_verification from the outset, with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required' (151 chars, +53 chars). 9+53 = 62 ≈ 61 (rounding).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — prose review. WIN-058b's underlying κ=1.677 ratio remains hardcoded-in-HTML as the c2 review established (no repository code computes it).
data_sources_used: claim_index.json — via transcribed quotes in detail_evidence; not re-fetched this run
chg-20260417-1620-004.json — referenced via WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 citation chain
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: ISS-1159's 'at least three' hedge is deliberately unspecified as to how many additional instances exist beyond the three enumerated. That is defensible (the hedge exists precisely because ai_manifest registry_semantics is a candidate fourth, to be documented via ISS-1087's eventual resolution), but without at least a parenthetical pointer, a dome defender can reasonably ask 'at least three... named three... where's the fourth?'. This is a known deferral in EXP-181 holes_deferred and is not fatal, but the unhedged opening sentence ('This is the third') is still a live contradiction.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by patches. Both patches are surgical textual edits. The two carry-forward citations in this WIN (onclick='showTab(\'wins\');return false' on the WIN-012 reference, and onclick='showTab(\'timestamp\');return false' on the Timestamp Error reference) retain the return-false-cancels-anchor-jump pattern separately filed as a new ISS-1158 in closed-issues (status: fixed). Verify that fix propagated to WIN-058b's two showTab anchors on next run — current wins.json still has the old pattern.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No API/fetch call for WIN-058b in monitor.py or pull_data.py. The κ=1.677 ratio, the r=0.992 correlation, and the Exhibit A statistic are all static HTML on the dome home page — no script computes them. Consistent with c2 tag.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: A universal magnetic-gravity coupling constant has no direct standard-physics analog — this WIN is genuinely novel in *framing* even though the phenomenon it points at (correlated SG/magnetometer signal during G5 storms) IS a known standard-physics result (shared electromagnetic forcing, ground-induced current artifacts per Crossley et al. 2013). The WIN frames the correlation as evidence of a new coupling mechanism, not as a relabeling of a known mechanism — so relabels_standard=false is correct. Consistent with c2.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The October 2003 Halloween-storm SG/magnetometer data was published in the early 2000s; the dome's κ=1.677 'prediction' is a ratio extracted from that prior data rather than a forward prediction of unseen measurements. The detail_evidence acknowledges this directly ('A ratio extracted from one event is a measurement, not a prediction'). post_hoc=true consistent with c2.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No equation in the dome model derives κ=1.677 from disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude, moon_altitude, or any other dome-specific parameter. The constant is 10.9/6.5 (the ratio of two measured quantities during one event), and the dome's own pages do not present a derivation from geometry. derives_from_dome=false consistent with c2.

holes_found.description: Internal inconsistency inside the patched Pattern paragraph. The paragraph now opens with 'This is the third acknowledge-without-fix move the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17, alongside [two named concessions]' — a definite count of three — and closes with 'At least three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window' — a hedged count allowing more. The ISS-1159 patch applied the hedge only to the closing sentence and left the opening sentence unchanged. A dome defender reading the paragraph top-to-bottom encounters the definite claim first and the hedge second, which reads as a contradiction rather than a considered hedge. Compare WIN-058's Pattern paragraph (patched in the same commit), which was hedged in BOTH places: heading 'at least three instances in commit 6e06efb' and body 'This is one of at least three parallel documentary concession[s]'. WIN-058b received only half the pass.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the same dual-hedge on WIN-058b as was applied to WIN-058. Suggested edit: change 'This is the third acknowledge-without-fix move the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17, alongside the methodology.json timestamp_scope concession...and the methodology.json registry_counts self-caveat (open issue ISS-1087).' to 'This is one of at least three acknowledge-without-fix moves the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17, alongside the methodology.json timestamp_scope concession...and the methodology.json registry_counts self-caveat (open issue ISS-1087; ai_manifest.json registry_semantics is a fourth parallel self-caveat in the same commit window and will be addressed in the ISS-1087 patch).' That preserves ISS-1159's hedge, aligns the paragraph with itself, and makes the 'at least' count defensible by naming the fourth candidate explicitly. One-line patch.

holes_found.description: Redundancy in the patched credit paragraph. After quoting the verbatim registry_integrity_notes text and stating 'This κ-ratio claim is now keyed win-058-bedrock-kappa (status: pending_independent_verification; claim_class: pending_contested; domain: gravity_magnetic_coupling). The API entry carries its own honest_caveat: "Independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required."', the patched sentence continues: '...the API no longer conflates the θ-rule with this coupling ratio, and the new claim_key registers this entry as pending_independent_verification from the outset, with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required.' The second clause restates the status+caveat information already presented two sentences earlier. 'We credit both moves' then frames this as if there are two distinct concessions, but move 2 (pending-from-the-outset + honest caveat) is really one concession already documented by the two preceding sentences. Not factually wrong — just slightly inflated. Readable, but a defender could push back on 'both moves' as double-counting. Minor because verdict, tags, and scientific content are unaffected.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional tightening. One option: drop the restatement: 'That is genuine documentary progress — the API no longer conflates the θ-rule with this coupling ratio. We credit the acknowledgement.' This preserves the credit and removes redundancy without reintroducing the 'downgraded' language. Another option: keep 'both moves' and explicitly enumerate them differently, e.g. 'We credit two moves: the disambiguation (separate claim_keys) and the honest_caveat requirement.' Lowest priority — wait until the next substantive edit of this paragraph before batching with other cleanup.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from prior review (not newly introduced): the two onclick='showTab(...);return false' anchors in WIN-058b (WIN-012 reference and Timestamp Error reference) retain the pattern where the 'return false' cancels the default anchor jump while still switching the tab. A reader who clicks 'WIN-012' lands at the top of the Wins tab rather than at the WIN-012 panel; a reader who clicks 'Timestamp Error tab' lands at the top of that tab rather than at id='timestamp-error'. A new ISS-1158 was filed for this (status: fixed in closed-issues.json) but the fix has not yet propagated to WIN-058b's two anchors in the current wins.json. Not new, not introduced by the c3 patches — flagging only so it's not lost.
holes_found.recommendation: On next edit pass, remove 'return false' from both onclick handlers in WIN-058b (and in all other WIN blocks that share the pattern) so the default anchor jump runs after the tab switch. Verify the destination anchors (id='win012', id='timestamp-error') land at a readable position in the switched-tab DOM before batching.

stronger_arguments.description: If the reviewer wants to preempt the 'at least three / named three' pushback more aggressively than a parenthetical, the Pattern paragraph could explicitly enumerate the fourth instance (ai_manifest.json registry_semantics), with the one-line pointer to ISS-1087. That converts a hedge-without-evidence into a hedge-with-enumeration, which is stronger rhetorically. Downside: expands the paragraph's length and gives the dome author an easy target to contest ('ai_manifest registry_semantics was already self-caveated — it's the same concession as registry_counts, not a parallel fourth'). The ISS-1087 resolution will settle this, so leaving the hedge unpopulated until then is defensible.
sources: monitor/decisions/open-issues.json ISS-1087 (94.5% bookkeeping-vs-audit split, still open)

stronger_arguments.description: The core post-hoc critique of κ=1.677 would be strengthened by a direct quote from the dome site showing that the 10.9 and 6.5 values (the numerator and denominator of 10.9/6.5 = 1.677) were both known in 2003-era literature before the dome model incorporated them. Right now the detail_evidence says 'A ratio extracted from one event is a measurement, not a prediction' but doesn't trace the individual values to their observational provenance. If we can show 10.9 μGal gravity residuals and 6.5 nT magnetic deltas were independently published in peer-reviewed 2003 Halloween-storm analyses before the dome first labeled κ=1.677 as a prediction, that closes any 'the dome predicted 1.67 before anyone measured it' counterargument completely. Useful for Tier 3 August 2026 pre-reg.
sources: Halloween 2003 superconducting gravimeter literature (Crossley, Hinderer et al.)
dome git history first mention of κ=1.677 (PRED-KAPPA-001 registration date)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author did identify the 'WIN-058 means two different claims' integrity issue our review had been flagging and shipped a machine-readable claim_index.json entry inside the 24-hour reactive wave. The honest_caveat on win-058-bedrock-kappa is a genuine documentary concession, and the claim_class (pending_contested, pending_independent_verification) is internally consistent with the κ=1.677 not being an automated monitoring target. Credit for those moves is earned.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Credit for machine-layer disambiguation does not rehabilitate the underlying κ=1.677 WIN: it remains a post-hoc single-event ratio with no derivation from dome geometry, no repository code to compute it, and a correlation figure (r=0.992) that shared-storm-driver physics produces independently of any dome coupling mechanism. The Monte Carlo rebuttal and the 'no repository code computes either the r-value or the 10.9/6.5 ratio' critique both survive the patches unchanged. Verdict Not Demonstrated stands.

deeper_analysis: This review's scope is narrow — post-patch verification of the ISS-1158 and ISS-1159 edits applied in commit a0f217d — but the finding is substantive: the ISS-1159 'at least three' hedge was applied asymmetrically. WIN-058 received both a heading hedge ('at least three instances in commit 6e06efb') and a body hedge ('one of at least three parallel'). WIN-058b received only the trailing hedge, leaving the Pattern paragraph's opening definite-count claim intact. A dome defender who reads WIN-058b top-to-bottom encounters the contradiction in two adjacent sentences, which is rhetorically worse than either unhedged ('three') or fully-hedged ('at least three' throughout). The fix is a one-line patch that brings WIN-058b in line with WIN-058's treatment. Recommend batching this with the minor redundancy fix in the credit paragraph so the detail_evidence gets one clean consolidation pass rather than two sequential c4/c5 cycles. Adversarial Advocate mode: a sharp dome defender would note (a) the internal inconsistency as evidence that the critique is being edited reactively rather than structurally, and (b) the 'both moves' framing as over-crediting relative to the singular concession actually documented. Neither defense neutralizes the underlying verdict, but both cost credibility. Quantitative Verification: confirmed detail_evidence_length=5813 matches expected delta from batch fingerprint 5752 + 61 chars; confirmed both patches present verbatim in current wins.json; confirmed no post-a0f217d modifications to WIN-058b via git log. Cross-WIN Consistency: WIN-058 has the fuller hedge treatment; WIN-062 (queue_id 184, not yet reviewed this run) should also be spot-checked for the same asymmetry when its turn arrives, but the decider's commit message describes only one patch for WIN-062 (ISS-1159 'third'->'one-of-at-least-three'), which if landed correctly is the cleaner treatment.

citations_verified: claim_index.json verbatim: 'Two different claims share this visible label in wins.html. The API disambiguates them as win-058-bedrock-kappa and win-058-theta-rule.' — quoted correctly in detail_evidence
honest_caveat verbatim: 'Independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required.' — quoted correctly
commit a0f217d patches both match recommendations from WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 holes #2 and #3 (ISS-1158, ISS-1159)
citations_failed: 'This is the third acknowledge-without-fix move' — this framing is not itself a citation to an external source, but is inconsistent with the ISS-1159 hedge applied three sentences later. Internal-consistency failure flagged as moderate hole #1.
citations_unchecked: Whether the live 2026-04-18 wins.html '69 confirmed' tally is still accurate at the time of this review (no re-fetch performed)

Whether 10.9 μGal and 6.5 nT (the numerator and denominator of the κ=1.677 ratio) were independently published in 2003-era peer-reviewed literature before the dome model incorporated them — relevant to stronger_arguments[1] but not to the patch-verification scope of this review

tldr_review.tldr_notes: Both TLDRs describe the original scientific critique (post-hoc ratio, shared-storm-forcing explanation of r=0.992, label collision) and are unaffected by the ISS-1158/1159 patches. No factual errors. The TLDRs do not mention the registry_integrity_notes acknowledgement or the Pattern paragraph, which is appropriate for a TLDR (readers see the summary; the EXP-181 discussion is for readers who expand the detail block). No change recommended.

cross_win_consistency.verdict_consistency: WIN-058b verdict Not Demonstrated unchanged. WIN-058 (queue_id 182, already reviewed in c4) verdict Misleading unchanged. WIN-062 (queue_id 184, not yet reviewed this run) verdict Misleading.
cross_win_consistency.tag_consistency: All four tags (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=false, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false) are unchanged by the patches and remain correct per c2 review.
cross_win_consistency.pattern_paragraph_hedge_asymmetry: WIN-058 Pattern paragraph: fully hedged (heading 'at least three instances in commit 6e06efb' AND body 'one of at least three parallel documentary concession'). WIN-058b Pattern paragraph: partially hedged (closing 'At least three documentary concessions' only; opening 'This is the third acknowledge-without-fix move' unhedged). This asymmetry is the primary moderate hole flagged in this review.
cross_win_consistency.cross_reference_link_health: WIN-058b -> WIN-058 link (#win058, no onclick): WORKING. WIN-058b -> WIN-012 link (#win012 with onclick='showTab(\'wins\');return false'): tab switch works, anchor jump cancelled (same UX issue flagged in closed-issues ISS-1158). WIN-058b -> Timestamp Error tab link (#timestamp-error with onclick='showTab(\'timestamp\');return false'): same pattern, same UX issue.
